key_insight: A fourth-order 3D singularly perturbed problem is split into easier second-order subproblems and a robust Stokes-type mixed system using a generalized Helmholtz decomposition.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical, low-order finite element route for solving thin-structure or boundary-layer-dominated PDEs with stability that does not deteriorate as the perturbation parameter becomes small.
method: Decompose the original fourth-order equation into two standard elliptic problems plus a curl-free constrained Stokes-type system.
Discretize the elliptic subproblems with standard linear finite elements.
Use the MINI element for the mixed system and add an L2 Lagrange-multiplier term to improve parameter-robustness.
evidence_signal: The paper reports a uniform error estimate of order h^{1/2} with respect to the perturbation parameter and supports the theory with numerical experiments.
watch_item: The convergence rate is only first-half order, so fine meshes may still be needed for high-accuracy engineering predictions.
paper_title: A Decoupled Low-Order Conforming Mixed Finite Element Method for a Three-Dimensional Fourth-Order Singularly Perturbed Problem
